name: Linguine with Scallops
recipeIngredient: 1 tablespoon Olive Oil or cooking oil
recipeInstructions:
1. Thaw scallops if frozen.
2. Halve any large scallops; set aside.
3. Cook linguine according to package directions.
4. Meanwhile, heat margarine and oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
5. Add scallops to the skillet; cook and stir for about 2 minutes or until opaque.
6. Remove scallops with a slotted spoon, leaving juices in the skillet.
7. Stir broth, white wine or vermouth, and lemon juice into the skillet.
8. Bring mixture to a boil. Reduce heat to medium-low and simmer for 10-12 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the liquid is reduced to about 1 cup.
9. Stir in onion, parsley, capers, and dillweed. Simmer uncovered for an additional minute.
10. Add scallops back into the skillet; stir gently to heat through.
11. Pour the sauce over the linguine and toss gently to combine.
Per serving: 362 calories, 28 g protein, 54 g carbohydrates, 4 g fat, 40 mg cholesterol, 407 mg sodium, 619 mg potassium.
